error.signature: Stripe webhook event received multiple times despite 200 response

verdict.summary: Stripe retries webhooks if your endpoint takes >5s to respond 200, even if it eventually succeeds. Docs say 'up to 3 days' but don't clarify the 5s window clearly.

dead_ends.action: Return 200 after processing the event fully
dead_ends.why_fails: If processing takes >5s, Stripe assumes failure and retries. You get duplicate events with same event ID.
Stripe explicitly guarantees 'at-least-once' delivery. Duplicates are expected behavior, not a bug.

workarounds.action: Return 200 immediately, then process asynchronously via queue
workarounds.how: Receive webhook -> store raw event in DB/queue -> return 200 -> process from queue

workarounds.action: Implement idempotency using event.id as dedup key
Use Stripe's event retrieval API to verify event authenticity instead of trusting payload
workarounds.how: event = stripe.Event.retrieve(event_id) # re-fetch from Stripe API
